author: mark
text:
Document bootstrapping a bare central hub from scratch

The bare hub is the recommended deployment, but no committed doc said how one comes to exist, and the README claimed the install section set it up when it does not. Investigation of Store::init confirmed bl init is not bare-aware: it resolves the root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel (a bare repo has none), and even past that, setup_state_branch/commit_init write and commit at the working-tree root, impossible at a bare root. Discovery is bare-aware (bl-8cf7); creation is not.

Added a 'Bootstrapping a bare hub from scratch' subsection under The bare central hub with the canonical sequence: bl init in a throwaway working-tree clone to create+push balls/tasks, git clone --bare into <hub>/.git, then reconstruct the loose store (state worktree, .balls/tasks symlink, .balls/local scaffolding, config.json materialized from main's tree since a bare root has no checkout). Verified against the live hub's on-disk layout. Fixed the false install-section cross-reference, added a bare-hub note to the bl init command section and the install Bare core bullet, and reconciled SPEC §10 (bl init is working-tree-only by design). Filed bl-9e8a (child) to track a first-class bl init --bare wrapper rather than leaving the gap papered over with manual-only instructions.
